title: Weekly wage at a standard hourly rate
problem: Sarah works $38$ hours in a week and is paid $\$24.50$ per hour. Calculate her weekly wage.

steps.instruction: Identify the hourly rate and the number of hours worked.
Use the formula: weekly wage $=$ hours worked $\times$ hourly rate.
Multiply to find the weekly wage.

title: Pay including time-and-a-half overtime
problem: Tom earns $\$22.00$ per hour for a standard $38$-hour week. He works $4$ hours overtime at time-and-a-half. What is his total weekly pay?

steps.instruction: Calculate standard pay for the 38 hours.
Find the overtime rate. Time-and-a-half means $1.5 \times$ the normal rate.
Add standard pay and overtime pay to find total weekly pay.

title: Fortnightly income with weekend and public holiday penalty rates
problem: Priya works $76$ standard hours in a fortnight at $\$28.50$ per hour. She also works $6$ hours on Saturday at time-and-a-half and $3$ hours on a public holiday at double time. Calculate her fortnightly gross income.

steps.instruction: Find the Saturday rate (time-and-a-half) and calculate Saturday pay.
Find the public holiday rate (double time) and calculate public holiday pay.
steps.working:
$$\text{Public holiday rate} = \$28.50 \times 2 = \$57.00 \text{ per hour}$$
$$\text{Public holiday pay} = 3 \times \$57.00 = \$171.00$$

steps.instruction: Add all three components to find the fortnightly gross income.
